article:
In brief: Today's weather in Abuja is quite warm and humid, with a temperature of 24°C that feels more like 29°C due to the high humidity level of 90%. As we look ahead to the next seven days, we can expect temperatures to rise significantly, with highs reaching up to 37°C by the end of the week. Rain chances will also increase, especially from Sunday onwards, so it's a good idea to plan your outdoor activities accordingly.

The current conditions are quite typical for this time of year in Abuja, with the heat and humidity making it feel quite oppressive. If you're planning on visiting the National Mosque or the National Assembly Complex today, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade if possible. The UV index is currently at 0, which is quite unusual, but this is likely due to the thick cloud cover that's been present over the city this morning.

As we take a look at the satellite imagery, [MAP: Satellite image of Abuja], we can see that there are some clouds gathering to the west of the city, which could potentially bring some rain later in the day. However, for now, it's still quite dry and sunny, so it's a good time to get out and enjoy the outdoors before the heat becomes too much.

### 2026-04-04
Tomorrow is looking like it will be a scorcher, with highs reaching up to 32°C and lows of 24°C. The rain chance is relatively low at 23%, but we can't rule out the possibility of some isolated thunderstorms popping up in the afternoon. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja National Park, be sure to bring plenty of water and sun protection, as the heat and sun can be quite intense.

As we head into the day, it's likely that the sun will start to break through the clouds, making it feel even hotter than it already is. The humidity will remain high, so it's a good idea to stay indoors during the hottest part of the day if possible. If you do need to venture out, try to stick to the shaded areas, such as the pedestrian walkways along Herbert Macaulay Way.

### 2026-04-05
On Saturday, we can expect even hotter temperatures, with highs reaching up to 34°C and lows of 26°C. The rain chance is relatively low at 13%, but we can't rule out the possibility of some isolated storms popping up in the late afternoon. If you're planning on attending any outdoor events, such as the Abuja Carnival, be sure to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade if possible.

As we take a look at the forecast models, [MAP: Forecast model image], we can see that there's a bit of a disagreement between the different models, with some predicting a higher chance of rain than others. However, overall, it's looking like it will be a dry and sunny day, with plenty of heat and humidity.

### 2026-04-06
On Sunday, we can expect the heat to continue, with highs reaching up to 35°C and lows of 26°C. The rain chance is starting to increase, with a 33% chance of precipitation. This could be in the form of isolated thunderstorms or more widespread rain, so it's a good idea to keep an eye on the skies and be prepared for changing weather conditions.

As we head into the day, it's likely that the clouds will start to build up, making it feel more humid and oppressive. The winds will remain light, at around 2 km/h, which will do little to provide any relief from the heat. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja Market, be sure to bring an umbrella, as the rain could start to fall at any time.

### 2026-04-07
On Monday, we can expect the rain chance to increase even further, with a 40% chance of precipitation. The highs will still be quite hot, reaching up to 34°C, but the lows will remain relatively steady at 26°C. This could be a good day to stay indoors and avoid the heat and humidity, especially if you're not fond of rain.

As we take a look at the radar imagery, [MAP: Radar image], we can see that there are already some areas of precipitation starting to build up to the west of the city. This could be a sign of things to come, with the rain potentially moving into the city later in the day.

### 2026-04-08
On Tuesday, we can expect the rain chance to reach its highest point of the week, with a 63% chance of precipitation. The highs will still be quite hot, reaching up to 34°C, but the lows will start to dip, reaching down to 25°C. This could be a good day to stay indoors and avoid the rain, especially if you're not fond of getting wet.

As we head into the day, it's likely that the rain will start to fall in earnest, with some areas potentially seeing heavy downpours. The winds will remain light, but the clouds will be thick and grey, making it feel quite gloomy. If you're planning on visiting the National Arboretum, be sure to bring an umbrella, as the rain could be quite heavy at times.

### 2026-04-09
On Wednesday, we can expect the rain chance to start to decrease, with a 51% chance of precipitation. The highs will still be quite hot, reaching up to 31°C, but the lows will remain steady at 25°C. This could be a good day to get out and enjoy the outdoors, as the rain will be starting to clear and the sun will be shining through the clouds.

As we take a look at the satellite imagery, [MAP: Satellite image], we can see that the clouds are starting to clear, making way for some sunshine to break through. The winds will remain light, but the humidity will start to decrease, making it feel more pleasant outdoors. If you're planning on visiting the Abuja Zoo, be sure to bring sun protection, as the sun can be quite strong.

### 2026-04-10
On Thursday, we can expect the heat to return, with highs reaching up to 37°C and lows of 25°C. The rain chance will be around 45%, which is still quite high, but the sun will be shining through the clouds, making it feel quite hot and humid. This could be a good day to stay indoors and avoid the heat, especially if you're not fond of the sun.

As we head into the day, it's likely that the sun will start to break through the clouds, making it feel even hotter than it already is. The humidity will remain high, so it's a good idea to stay hydrated and take breaks in the shade if possible. If you're planning on visiting the National Museum, be sure to bring plenty of water and sun protection, as the heat and sun can be quite intense.
